# 'gs branch squash --fill' uses the configured message generator
# to produce the squash commit message.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-03-30T14:59:32Z'

cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo i

# Create a branch with two commits
git add feature1.txt
gs branch create feature-1 -m 'First commit'
git add feature2.txt
git commit -m 'Second commit'

# configure the message generator
git config spice.messageGenerator 'echo generated squash message'

# squash with --fill
gs branch squash --fill

# verify the commit message
git log -1 --format=%s
stdout 'generated squash message'

-- repo/feature1.txt --
Feature 1
-- repo/feature2.txt --
Feature 2
